name: Dorothea Orem
practice: Self-care deficit nursing theory, understanding when people can and cannot care for themselves
approach: Self-care as the baseline. The system intervenes only when self-care capacity is exceeded. The goal is always to restore capacity, not create dependency.
body_of_work: Developed the Self-Care Deficit Theory of Nursing (1971). Three interconnected theories: self-care, self-care deficit, and nursing systems. Framework for understanding when support is needed and when it should withdraw.
why_instructive: Her framework maps directly to dome layer 10 (Autonomy). The dome's goal isn't to provide everything — it's to identify where self-care capacity breaks down and bridge only those gaps.

dome_relevance.4: Health theory — nursing science and care planning
dome_relevance.10: Autonomy — her entire framework is about when to support and when to step back
